symptom_description: Phase-to-phase flashover occurred across main busbar support insulators during humid morning startup (`minor` soot deposits across backplane). Tech observed abnormal acoustic noise propagating across the coupling from XFMR-402.
root_cause: Entry of rodents/pests into distribution cabinet through unsealed floor conduits caused direct phase-to-ground short across energized bus.
action_taken: Replaced tracked busbar standoff insulators, cleaned copper bus surfaces, installed new polycarbonate phase barriers, and verified insulation resistance across all phases before re-energization.
parts_used: Bus Support Insulator Assembly Kit, Heavy-Wall Busbar Heat Shrink Tubing (10ft), Duct Seal Compound (2 Plugs)

symptom_description: Routine secondary injection testing revealed feeder breaker long-time and short-time trip units failed to actuate within specified curve (`> 500%` time delay).
root_cause: Shunt trip coil burnout (`open circuit`) preventing remote safety interlock system from opening breaker.
action_taken: Replaced complete draw-out air circuit breaker (`ACB`) assembly, renewed bus terminal stabs, and verified all protection settings against engineering coordination study.
parts_used: Electronic Trip Unit Module (ETU Rebuild Kit), Synthetic Breaker Lubricating Grease (1 Tube)

symptom_description: Phase A terminal lug glowing red under peak shift current (`69` Amps). Thermal imaging shows `112°C` temperature at cable-to-bus bolted joint. Tech observed abnormal acoustic noise propagating across the coupling from XFMR-402.
root_cause: Galvanic corrosion and oxidation between dissimilar metals (`copper cable to aluminum bus lug`) causing high joint contact resistance.
action_taken: Cleaned arced busbar contact surfaces, installed new silver-plated copper splice plates and high-tensile hardware with Belleville washers, and verified micro-ohm contact resistance (`< 15 micro-ohms`).
parts_used: Heavy-Duty Copper Compression Lugs (Set of 3), Belleville Spring Washers & Grade 8 Bolts, Conductive Electrical Joint Compound (1 Tube)

symptom_description: Loud excessive buzzing and arcing sound (`grinding`) heard inside distribution panel enclosure. Localized persistent discoloration on bus connection bolts.
root_cause: Thermal expansion and contraction cycles over years of fluctuating load current caused gradual loosening of bolted busbar and cable lug mechanical terminations.
action_taken: Replaced heat-damaged Phase A and B terminal lugs, polished copper bus interfaces, applied electrical joint compound, torqued hardware to specification, and re-scanned with IR camera under load (`joint temp stabilized at 38°C`).
parts_used: Heavy-Duty Copper Compression Lugs (Set of 3), Belleville Spring Washers & Grade 8 Bolts, Conductive Electrical Joint Compound (1 Tube)

symptom_description: Voltage imbalance of `sharp` across panel busbars under heavy load. Noticeable smell of heated ozone/insulation near panel vents. Tech observed abnormal acoustic noise propagating across the coupling from XFMR-402.
root_cause: Galvanic corrosion and oxidation between dissimilar metals (`copper cable to aluminum bus lug`) causing high joint contact resistance.
action_taken: Replaced heat-damaged Phase A and B terminal lugs, polished copper bus interfaces, applied electrical joint compound, torqued hardware to specification, and re-scanned with IR camera under load (`joint temp stabilized at 38°C`).
parts_used: Silver-Plated Busbar Splice Plates (2-Pack), Anti-Oxidation Compound Paste, High-Tensile Hardware Kit

symptom_description: Phase A terminal lug glowing red under peak shift current (`210` Amps). Thermal imaging shows `112°C` temperature at cable-to-bus bolted joint.
root_cause: Thermal expansion and contraction cycles over years of fluctuating load current caused gradual loosening of bolted busbar and cable lug mechanical terminations.
action_taken: Replaced heat-damaged Phase A and B terminal lugs, polished copper bus interfaces, applied electrical joint compound, torqued hardware to specification, and re-scanned with IR camera under load (`joint temp stabilized at 38°C`).
parts_used: Heavy-Duty Copper Compression Lugs (Set of 3), Belleville Spring Washers & Grade 8 Bolts, Conductive Electrical Joint Compound (1 Tube)

symptom_description: Infrared (`IR`) thermography inspection revealed severe hotspot (`102`°C, `delta-T > 45°C above ambient`) on Phase B main busbar lug termination.
root_cause: Vibration transmitted from nearby heavy manufacturing equipment loosened bolted terminal connections on main bus joints over time.
action_taken: Replaced heat-damaged Phase A and B terminal lugs, polished copper bus interfaces, applied electrical joint compound, torqued hardware to specification, and re-scanned with IR camera under load (`joint temp stabilized at 38°C`).

symptom_description: Voltage imbalance of `minor` across panel busbars under heavy load. Noticeable smell of heated ozone/insulation near panel vents. Field diagnostics indicate misalignment at shaft coupling connecting directly to XFMR-402.
root_cause: Improper initial torque application during installation (`bolts tightened without calibrated torque wrench or Belleville spring washers`).
action_taken: Cleaned arced busbar contact surfaces, installed new silver-plated copper splice plates and high-tensile hardware with Belleville washers, and verified micro-ohm contact resistance (`< 15 micro-ohms`).

symptom_description: Main distribution panel tripped incoming feeder phase-to-ground fault protection. Strong excessive smell of ionized electrical arc and smoke inside cabinet.
root_cause: Entry of rodents/pests into distribution cabinet through unsealed floor conduits caused direct phase-to-ground short across energized bus.
action_taken: De-energized panel, replaced flashover-damaged bus support insulators and phase barriers, applied heat-shrink insulating tubing over exposed buswork, and sealed conduit entry points with duct seal.
parts_used: Bus Support Insulator Assembly Kit, Heavy-Wall Busbar Heat Shrink Tubing (10ft), Duct Seal Compound (2 Plugs)

symptom_description: Voltage imbalance of `marked` across panel busbars under heavy load. Noticeable smell of heated ozone/insulation near panel vents.
root_cause: Vibration transmitted from nearby heavy manufacturing equipment loosened bolted terminal connections on main bus joints over time.
action_taken: Replaced heat-damaged Phase A and B terminal lugs, polished copper bus interfaces, applied electrical joint compound, torqued hardware to specification, and re-scanned with IR camera under load (`joint temp stabilized at 38°C`).

symptom_description: Main distribution panel tripped incoming feeder phase-to-ground fault protection. Strong intermittent smell of ionized electrical arc and smoke inside cabinet. High load demand from driven equipment (XFMR-402) suspected of accelerating component wear.
root_cause: Accumulation of conductive industrial dust, carbon soot, and high ambient moisture across busbar standoff insulators formed a tracking path to ground.
action_taken: Replaced cracked standoff insulators, re-torqued bus supports, thoroughly vacuumed and solvent cleaned panel cabinet, and installed rodent screens on all cabinet ventilation louvers.
parts_used: Glastic/GPO-3 Busbar Standoff Insulators (6-Pack), Dielectric Solvent Cleaner (3 Cans), Polycarbonate Phase Barriers (Set of 3)

symptom_description: Ops reported breaker handle stuck in middle/tripped position (`sharp` resistance). Internal mechanism clicking but contacts will not latch closed.
root_cause: Mechanical binding and hardening of factory grease inside breaker operating mechanism due to lack of periodic exercising over 10+ years.
action_taken: Swapped out jammed circuit breaker assembly, installed new shunt trip accessory coil, torqued busbar connections to 275 in-lbs, and performed insulation resistance test.
parts_used: Replacement MCCB 600A Assembly, Secondary Injection Test Plug Adapter

symptom_description: Main distribution panel tripped incoming feeder phase-to-ground fault protection. Strong gradual smell of ionized electrical arc and smoke inside cabinet.
root_cause: Transient overvoltage strike exceeded dielectric breakdown limit of degraded busbar shrink-tubing insulation.
action_taken: De-energized panel, replaced flashover-damaged bus support insulators and phase barriers, applied heat-shrink insulating tubing over exposed buswork, and sealed conduit entry points with duct seal.
parts_used: GPO-3 Insulator Set, Bus Mount Bolts, Dielectric Cleaning Solvent

symptom_description: Phase A terminal lug glowing red under peak shift current (`178` Amps). Thermal imaging shows `112°C` temperature at cable-to-bus bolted joint.
root_cause: Vibration transmitted from nearby heavy manufacturing equipment loosened bolted terminal connections on main bus joints over time.
action_taken: Replaced heat-damaged Phase A and B terminal lugs, polished copper bus interfaces, applied electrical joint compound, torqued hardware to specification, and re-scanned with IR camera under load (`joint temp stabilized at 38°C`).

symptom_description: Loud abnormal buzzing and arcing sound (`clicking`) heard inside distribution panel enclosure. Localized high discoloration on bus connection bolts.
root_cause: Vibration transmitted from nearby heavy manufacturing equipment loosened bolted terminal connections on main bus joints over time.
action_taken: De-energized main panel, performed complete torquing check across all main bus connections and feeder breaker terminals using calibrated torque wrench, and applied anti-oxidation paste.
parts_used: Replacement Terminal Lugs 500 MCM, Hardware & Washer Kit, Electrical Contact Cleaner

symptom_description: Phase A terminal lug glowing red under peak shift current (`75` Amps). Thermal imaging shows `112°C` temperature at cable-to-bus bolted joint.
root_cause: Galvanic corrosion and oxidation between dissimilar metals (`copper cable to aluminum bus lug`) causing high joint contact resistance.
action_taken: Scheduled emergency shutdown, disassembled Phase B cable lug and busbar joint, cleaned oxidized contact faces with Scotch-Brite, applied conductive joint compound, and torqued bolts to factory spec (`275 in-lbs`) using Belleville washers.
parts_used: Replacement Terminal Lugs 500 MCM, Hardware & Washer Kit, Electrical Contact Cleaner
